Spot Risky Generics

A generic should preserve information. If it mostly hides a cast, review it as a risk.

Smell: generic in, assertion out

export function parseJson<T>(text: string): T {
  return JSON.parse(text) as T;
}

const settings = parseJson<{ retries: number }>(rawSettings);
settings.retries.toFixed();

The caller picks T, so the function does not prove anything about the parsed value. It just makes the assertion reusable.

Better review question

Ask whether the code needs a schema, a narrower return type, or a call-site guard. Do not ask for "stronger typing" in the abstract.
